evals.prompt: I have Huabu running locally and want to generate a product hero image on a new canvas. Set up the project preflight, start the canvas agent run, and tell me what command to use to watch results.
evals.expected_output: Explains that a workspace project is required, uses or creates a project, starts /api/agent/canvas-runs with canvas_context.projectId, and watches SSE events.

evals.prompt: Use project_id 434d69ef-a16c-4ed0-a757-0ae51aa555ba and this image URL to ask the canvas agent to make a video from it. I need generated video URLs when it finishes.
evals.expected_output: Verifies or uses the provided project ID, passes the image URL as image_urls, creates a durable canvas run, streams events, and extracts generated_video_result/videoUrls.

evals.prompt: I pasted an /api/agent/canvas-runs/<id>/events URL from Huabu. Help me turn it into a reusable external automation flow for future canvas agent calls.
evals.expected_output: Warns about system token safety, identifies site-id 10000 plus token/project_id/run_id, stores only YUNJIAN_SYSTEM_TOKEN in local scripts/.env when provided, and describes project selection, run creation, and event streaming rather than only consuming the pasted event URL.

evals.prompt: The host canvas failed to apply a canvas_operation returned by the canvas agent. I need the external agent to report the real failure back and get a user-facing recovery message.
evals.expected_output: Uses /api/agent/canvas-chat with tool_result_feedback, keeps the same conversation_id and canvas_context.projectId, includes event_type, summary, operation, error, and results, and does not silently retry with guessed inputs.

evals.prompt: Build a robust image-to-video canvas-agent call. The user provided a .jpeg image and a .mp4 reference video. Make sure the payload does not mix media types.
evals.expected_output: Passes the .jpeg only in image_urls and the .mp4 only in video_urls, explains the media field separation, creates or reuses a project-scoped conversation, and starts a durable canvas run.

evals.prompt: While streaming a canvas run I received event: agent_skills before canvas_operation. What should my external integration do with it?
evals.expected_output: Treats agent_skills as diagnostic context about injected prompt skills, does not execute it as a canvas operation, continues streaming, and only executes canvas_operation events in the host canvas.
